name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: We all want to do more
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6760/24c69fd9/a90a0dc6/189b9b2e/3678380350/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE853N_digital_email_20170828_CainRundown_inact&source=WQEFE853N_digital_email_20170828_CainRundown_inact")
---

Peter,

The 2017-2018 election cycle is shaping up to be one of our biggest EVER.

We have already had 18x more women reach out to us than last cycle about running for office. We have put a record 50 anti-choice congressional Republicans "On Notice." And **49 women** across the country who have put their names on the ballot have received our endorsement.

But with Trump in the White House and spineless Republicans in Congress, we all want -- and need -- to do more.

**[Donate $3 to help meet our $125,000 End-of-Month goal and build on all we have accomplished so far this cycle-- and jumpstart the work we have still to do.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6760/24c69fd9/a90a0dc6/189b9b2f/3678380350/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170828_email_August-EOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+meet+our+125000+EndofMo&utm_campaign=WQEFE853N_digital_email_20170828_CainRundown_inact&source=WQEFE853N_digital_email_20170828_CainRundown_inact")**

This community has responded to unprecedented threats with unprecedented resistance.

We joined the Women's March -- the largest single day of protest in American history.

When Sen. Dean Heller cast the deciding vote to move Trumpcare forward, we rushed support to Jacky Rosen, the pro-choice Democratic woman who will defeat Heller next year.

And with every attack from the Trump administration on women, people of color, immigrants, the LGBTQ community, and so many others, it's been elected EMILY's List women leading the charge to fight back -- from Sen. Elizabeth Warren joining airport protests against the Muslim ban to Sen. Kamala Harris' dogged questioning of Jeff Sessions about Russia's election interference to literally everything Rep. Maxine Waters has said.

**[Donate $3 right now to help keep up the fight and meet our $125,000 End-of-Month goal for August.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6760/24c69fd9/a90a0dc6/189b9b28/3678380350/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170828_email_August-EOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Donate+3+right+now+to+help+keep+up+the+f&utm_campaign=WQEFE853N_digital_email_20170828_CainRundown_inact&source=WQEFE853N_digital_email_20170828_CainRundown_inact")**

Thank you,

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

name: The White House
subject: The President and the First Lady Travel to Texas
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=29b55953934431daf5997a6f11e4206c5a9b9ace7c71dc0611631a5ca9a3d79d4af3e59efce1da51645b5818e1639ff40d7daa1cf57fcf44

-------------

The President and the First Lady Travel to Texas

Today, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump will travel to Corpus Christi, Texas, where they will receive a briefing on Hurricane Harvey relief efforts with local leadership and relief organizations. In the afternoon, the President and the First Lady will tour the Emergency Operations Center in Austin, Texas.

President Trump Welcomes President Niinistö of Finland to the White House

On Monday, President Trump welcomed President Niinistö of Finland to the White House for a bilateral meeting and press conference.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=29b55953934431da2fe2d30b8699c0848d9a30406010eed86429d85868bb7c47d0712e4d63dc10b6a243fa0a3015a697b8b5c6e1f6842e66
Read more

Statement by President Trump on North Korea
"The world has received North Korea’s latest message loud and clear: this regime has signaled its contempt for its neighbors, for all members of the United Nations, and for minimum standards of acceptable international behavior. Threatening and destabilizing actions only increase the North Korean regime’s isolation in the region and among all nations of the world. All options are on the table." - President Donald J. Trump

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=29b55953934431da99d6fd4c479707e9d3e28b5f00544f3e17f8794a1bc716766da64425ffaaf4767584486f08d0182bdcda175b98887af5
Read more

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump depart for Texas | August 29, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS and VP Today

Today, President Trump and the First Lady will travel to Texas where they will meet with state and local leaders to discuss ongoing Federal support for Hurricane Harvey response and recovery.
Vice President Pence will have lunch with Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue.

White House Spring Internship

The application for the Spring 2018 White House Internship Program is open!

name: Tom Perez
subject: How you can help:
body:
Here's how you can help those affected by Harvey
---
_According to our records, you live outside of Texas. However, if you are currently in an area affected by Hurricane Harvey, Texas Democrats have compiled a list of useful resources**[here.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523613/4067b0a/3e5d83690/7e6e2740/977067366/VEsH/ "http://my.democrats.org/TX-Resources?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170829_tp_f2&source=em_20170829_tp_f2")**_

Derek --

Our thoughts and prayers go out to everyone who has been affected by Hurricane Harvey. This storm has done catastrophic damage across Texas, and there is more still to come.

As President Obama reminded us, helping each other out is what we do as Americans. National and local relief organizations are already on the ground in communities across Texas, and you can make an important difference at this critical moment, too.

**To learn more about how you can help, visit the Red Cross or Greater Houston Community Foundation. If you're able to, I hope you'll lend a hand:**

**[ RED CROSS](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523613/4067b0a/3e5d83690/7e6e2741/977067366/VEsE/ "http://my.democrats.org/Red-Cross?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+RED+CROSS&utm_campaign=em_20170829_tp_f2&source=em_20170829_tp_f2")
[GREATER HOUSTON COMMUNITY FOUNDATION](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523613/4067b0a/3e5d83690/7e6e274e/977067366/VEsF/ "http://my.democrats.org/GHCF?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+GREATER+HOUSTON+COMMUNITY+FOUNDATION&utm_campaign=em_20170829_tp_f2&source=em_20170829_tp_f2")
**
Thank you to everyone who has already stepped up to help our friends and neighbors in Texas, and a special thanks to our brave first responders who are working to keep folks safe.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523613/4067b0a/3e5d83690/7e6e274f/977067366/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170829_tp_f2&source=em_20170829_tp_f2"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523613/4067b0a/3e5d83690/7e6e274c/977067366/VEsD/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170829_tp_f2&source=em_20170829_tp_f2"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523613/4067b0a/3e5d83690/7e6e274d/977067366/VEsA/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170829_tp_f2&source=em_20170829_tp_f2") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523613/4067b0a/3e5d83690/7e6e274a/977067366/VEsB/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170829_tp_f2&source=em_20170829_tp_f2"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

subject: Let’s help our fellow Americans

body:
Peter,

Our fellow Americans need our help.

Far too many people have been left without food, water, shelter, or power. This is our moment to extend our hearts to the victims of this tragedy and to the silent heroes risking their lives to save others.

Below is a list of suggested charities President Trump emailed to our supporters earlier today. I’d now like to echo his call to help our fellow Americans by donating to one of these charities.

Let’s show the world the true compassion of the American people.

American Red Cross –

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: A fight bigger than Trump
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

Somebody recently asked me if I agreed that President Trump should be
impeached.

Well of course I agree with that. He simply has to go.

But the thing I want to emphasize is that the assault on our
democratic legitimacy is not just about Donald Trump. It’s been going
on for decades. If Mike Pence becomes president, the assault will
still happen, because this stuff was the product of the far right long
before Trump.

That’s why the work we do here at LegitAction is so important. In
order to continue funding that work at full strength, we need to raise
an additional $15,000 in grassroots donations by the end of the month.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/545?t=1002&akid=238%2E356062%2EfULqAH ]Contribute $3 today to help reach this
important $15,000 grassroots goal before the end of the month.

To see how far the problem goes, just look at what the rest of the
Republican Party is doing.

Some Republican leaders denounced Nazism after Charlottesville, but
where were the Republican leaders denouncing voter suppression as
racist, un-American, and dangerous?

Gerrymandering, strict voter ID laws, and felon disenfranchisement are
all aimed at one outcome: a voting class that is predominantly white,
and in turn majority Republican. And Republicans like Mike Pence were
pushing for this long before Trump arrived on the scene.

Help reach this grassroots goal before the end of the month, and
together we will keep fighting a fight that is bigger than Trump.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/545?t=1003&akid=238%2E356062%2EfULqAH ]Contribute $3 today to help reach this
important $15,000 grassroots goal before the end of the month.

Trump is a grave threat, and he absolutely must go. But we can’t let
people think the threat goes away when he does.

Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold

Team,
**A recent report from _The Hill_ lists Republican Dean Heller's Nevada Senate seat as the number one most likely to flip in 2018.**
Heller is the only Republican up for re-election in a state that Donald Trump lost, and Democrats have just the candidate to defeat him: Jacky Rosen.

Derek,

"On many sides." With these three words, President Trump sent a horrible signal that white supremacists were not responsible for the violence, hatred and bigotry we saw in Charlottesville. And while Americans of all backgrounds and political parties condemned President Trump's response, one voice rang out defending him: Corey Stewart.

We don't know yet if Corey is going to be the Republican nominee against Tim in 2018, but here's what we do know: He embraces the confederate flag, has campaigned with a white supremacist who organized the Charlottesville rally, and uses divisive rhetoric that pits Virginians against one another and empowers some of the worst elements of our society.

subject: wanted to get back to you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
|
---

| | Peter,

A few of days ago, we asked you to submit your questions to our virtual mailbag for the "Ask Joe!" Q&A. Joe wanted to meet all of you on his statewide tour last week, but we thought sitting down with him in the RV to answer a few of your mailbag questions was the next best thing: | [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c27a4/7377f838/37b67b06/51fbc25b/850561973/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-surveyfollowup?refcode=20170829&amount=10")
---
_Pictured: Joe reading your questions from the road during the RV Tour._
> **Q1. What are the Democrats planning to do to improve the healthcare system? Is there bipartisan work being done on this?**
>
> A: It's no secret that the ACA isn't perfect. There's still plenty of work to do to improve our system and make sure Hoosiers -- and all Americans -- get the care they deserve. But the recent strategy of repeal and replace is not the answer. That's why I've already started to work across the aisle -- meeting with senators from both parties to talk about ways to improve our existing system and help draft a bill that could pass. It's also imperative that the President must stop undermining the current health care law by creating uncertainty that is driving up cost, pushing insurers out of the market, and preventing American families from accessing affordable healthcare.
>
> **Q2. I am a disabled vet, not working anymore. What can you do to protect our Social Security and Veteran benefits?**
>
> A. We _must_ honor the promises we made to our seniors and our veterans. I intend to keep those promises by continuing to work with members of both parties to protect Social Security and expand Veterans benefits. I will continue to do everything I can to make sure Americans get the retirement and the benefits, that they've earned throughout their lives.
>
> **Q3. I work with high school students getting them registered to vote. Their #1 concern is college affordability. What is your plan?**
>
> A. If we're going to see Hoosiers get ahead, college affordability is something we're going to have to tackle. Young people are our future, and they deserve every opportunity to receive an education and attend a great university. That's why I've supported increased Pell Grant funding and worked to find ways to talk to students about financial literacy before they take out loans to pay for college.
>
> **Q4: What are three of your biggest priorities to help the Hoosier middle class?**
>
> A. The three biggest things I will fight to see done are:
> 1. Stability in our health care markets to stop Hoosier families' premiums from rising.
> 2. Stop the outsourcing of American jobs and ensure that taxpayer-funded, federal contracts go to companies that are investing in American workers and our communities here at home.
> 3. Address the opioid epidemic that is devastating communities across our state and the country.
>

Thanks so much for taking the time to submit your questions and words of support. Knowing that Hoosiers are invested in this campaign and have Joe's back means the world to him.

Please stay tuned for updates throughout the rest of our campaign.

Thank you,

Team Donnelly

P.S. Joe crisscrossed the state to hear from Hoosiers about what matters to them and get our message out to as many people as possible along the way. **[Running a campaign like this takes resources, will you pitch in to make sure we can keep reaching Hoosiers across Indiana?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c27a4/7377f838/37b67b06/51fbc25b/850561973/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-surveyfollowup?refcode=20170829&amount=10")**

name: Jim Hightower
subject: How to help Houston
body:
Wanted to make sure you saw this note from OR Texas on how people in Houston
#
Friends,
Wanted to make sure you saw this note from OR Texas on how people in Houston and around the country can help.
Thank you for helping to make a difference.
In solidarity,
Jim Hightower
Board Member
Our Revolution
\----------Forwarded message----------
From: Chris Kutalik-Cauthern via ActionNetwork.org
Date: Mon, August 28, 2017
Subject: Hurricane Harvey: How to Help
To: Jim Hightower
Friends,
Thousands in our state are suffering because of Hurricane Harvey and spillover flooding since this weekend. Our Revolution Texas members are among them. Many of us around the state are wondering how we can volunteer and donate to help the many in hard-hit areas. We all know that showing solidarity in trying times is an important part of what we do and why we do it as progressive populists.
**In the Houston Area**
If you have a flat-bottomed boat or a high-water vehicle, you are urgently needed. Call 713-881-3100.
If you are in need of immediate help from a life-threatening emergency call 9-1-1.
If you are or know others in need of non-emergency help call 3-1-1 in Houston or 2-1-1 in nearby areas.
**Outside Houston**
Local relief groups, progressive organizations, unions, and immigrant rights groups are already mobilizing grassroots relief efforts. Here is a list of groups and efforts you can plug into to help:
[**The Texas Workers Relief Fund**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7be/2992579386/VEsH/ "http://www.texasaflcio.org/donate"). A union-relief effort by the Texas AFL-CIO, donations are tax-deductible. The state fed has been closely coordinating with the Houston and Corpus-area central labor councils to provide material aid.
[**RNRN Disaster Relief Fund**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7b9/2992579386/VEsE/ "http://www.nationalnursesunited.org/pages/rnrn-disaster-relief-fund"). Our ally **the National Nurses United** organizes medical relief for major disasters through this fund.
**Texas DSA**. DSA chapters (also allies) have been organizing both volunteers and those in need at a grassroots level. Sign up [**here to offer help (or ask for it)**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7b8/2992579386/VEsF/ "https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeFqb_PcEkVyQ95KzDqOfm4Uwn2b_ZQbdggM33xtI_heh26gQ/viewform") or [**donate directly to Houston DSA**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7bb/2992579386/VEsC/ "https://www.youcaring.com/peopleimpactedbyhurricaneharvey-914409").
[**Coastal Bend Disaster Recovery Group Fund**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7ba/2992579386/VEsD/ "http://www.cbcfoundation.org/"). If you want to donate directly for relief in the Coastal Bend towns hit directly by the hurricane.
[**Hurricane Harvey Relief Fund**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7a5/2992579386/VEsA/ "https://ghcf.org/hurricane-relief/"). Houston's mayor has set up this fund to assist with victims of Houston's ongoing and increasingly dangerous flooding. Donations are tax-deductible.
The extreme enforcement policies of SB4 and ICE have put immigrant workers in increased harm's way through the crisis. Immigrant and refugee groups such as [**RAICES**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7a4/2992579386/VEsB/ "https://www.raicestexas.org/pages/volunteer-opportunities") in San Antonio are moving to get aid directly to immigrant families. Jumping in now is just the beginning.
In solidarity,
Chris Kutalik-Cauthern
Our Revolution Texas, Statewide Coordinator
PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002

Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7a6/2992579386/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjIyMTIzIn0=/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/OR-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7a1/2992579386/VEsHBQ/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Help us continue transforming American politics and fighting for progressive candidates and causes by [contributing to Our Revolution here](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af8f/bed43eb/9c9b7c1/75ffa7a0/2992579386/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-footer-OR").

name: Tom Perez
subject: re: Trump's chief lobbyist
body:
Step up before our deadline on Thursday.
---
**We need 68 Democrats from your community to step up before Thursday if we're going to hit our August fundraising goal. Can you chip in $3 or more today?

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052362c/4067b0a/3e49a0845/7e6e2eb9/1256942586/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/August-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170827_tp_nd&source=em_20170827_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170827_tp_nd")**

Derek --

There are just a few short weeks until Election Day, and we have a chance to hand Donald Trump a big defeat in Virginia.

The Republican candidate for governor, Ed Gillespie, is Trump's chief lobbyist. He touts his support for Trump on the trail, he's embracing the agenda of Betsy DeVos, and he invited Mike Pence to campaign with him. When Trump threatened to take hundreds of thousands of Virginians' health care away, Gillespie was silent.

The GOP is pulling out all the stops to win this race -- they're calling in their billionaire donors like the Koch brothers. And earlier this week, Virginia Republicans launched a vile, racist attack on Democrat Ralph Northam because he's standing on the right side of history after Charlottesville. They'll say and do anything to win.

We want to go all-in for Ralph and all of our great candidates. But we need your help to do it.

Thursday is our final fundraising deadline before the fall (get-out-the-vote season!), and we're still short of our goal. We need 68 grassroots supporters from your community to pitch in before then. Derek, can I count on you?

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052362c/4067b0a/3e49a0845/7e6e2e42/1256942586/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/august-Deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20170827_tp_nd&source=em_20170827_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170827_tp_nd")

body:

| |
---
| Friends,

This week marked the 25th anniversary of Hurricane Andrew. Tens of thousands of Florida families were displaced and suffered greatly from the resulting destruction.

I remember that in our time of need, friends and neighbors from across the country came to our aid. **Now, it is time for us to do the same for the people of Texas and Louisiana who have been devastated by Hurricane Harvey.**

Families lost their homes and all their possessions. Some have lost loved ones. And the rains and flooding continues.

[_**In their time of need, please consider donating to the Red Cross to support those impacted by Harvey.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e33/622b7b5b/59e2342/307bdaf1/2961170763/VEsH/ "https://www.redcross.org/donate/hurricane-harvey")

I am incredibly thankful for the dedication and hard work of first responders and volunteers helping those in harm's way. As we continue to witness all the destruction and devastation caused by Harvey, please keep all of those in harm's way in your thoughts.

Thank you,

Debbie |

Team --

As Senator Warren's former campaign manager, I can tell you this: Never underestimate the amount of resources you need to win -- or the uniquely vicious attacks that will be thrown at our tough, female candidates.

That's where the Women's Senate Network comes in. We're committed to helping nearly half of the women in the Senate build strong campaigns this cycle -- but we need your help to make it happen. So for a limited time you can get a "Nevertheless, She Persisted" tote bag with a donation of $35 more.

[**Pitch in $35 to get your tote and support the Women 's Senate Network -- these totes are going fast (Shipping included!)**](http://action.dscc. Next year **Mazie Hirono, Claire McCaskill, Elizabeth Warren, and seven other Democratic women are up for re-election.** These are women who have faced down some of the nastiest slurs and attacks but keep moving forward to victory.

If we want to keep them fighting for us in the Senate, they can't do it alone. They need our help. And they need it now.

Mindy

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: how low will he go?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __ During these first seven months of the Trump administration, I've thought many times that things couldn't get much worse. But after the events of Charlottesville and then his unconscionable pardon of Joe Arpaio, President Trump keeps sinking lower and lower. Every time we think he can't go any lower, he does. Donald Trump's defense of white supremacists and neo-Nazis is not only repugnant, it's a dangerous stance that will likely encourage more violence. His pardon of convicted racial profiler, disgraced former Sheriff Arpaio, sent a signal that Donald Trump will allow people to violate civil rights with impunity. This isn't something that can be swept under the rug. With Congress back in session a week from today, we must take this opportunity to speak out against Donald Trump's divisive rhetoric and policies, as well as those Republicans who simply stand by and refuse to denounce it. The entire world watched in horror as the president unapologetically winked at neo-Nazis, the KKK, and white supremacists. In fact, the United Nations took the extraordinary step of criticizing Trump's failure to condemn racist violence. Meanwhile, Trump is bringing his bigotry to his policymaking: banning patriotic and qualified transgender individuals from the military, threatening to end the DACA program that protects innocent Dreamers from being deported, and pardoning a sheriff who was convicted of criminal contempt for illegally hunting down and detaining immigrants. As we work overtime to prevent Trump from turning back the clock on progress in America, we must build our strength so we can win in next year's mid-term elections. I am proud to lead those efforts in the Senate as the Chair of the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee (DSCC) and thank you for supporting our efforts. Thanks for your continued generosity, Chris Van Hollen [DONATE HERE](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd6/70cdc2ba/4693a388/397de1d/2840538530/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cvh-june17?refcode=em082917&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+DONATE+HERE&utm_campaign=em082917&source=em082917")

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: It's time to lend a hand (Hurricane Harvey)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
|
---

| | Peter --

I'm reaching out today because the people of Texas and the Gulf Coast need us right now.

In what's being called a "500 year" storm, families are in desperate need of shelter and resources. Hurricane Harvey continues to wash out roads and destroy businesses, community centers, and homes. During the last week I've been incredibly proud, though not surprised, to see Hoosiers from all over the state quickly deploy to Texas to help with rescue and recovery efforts.

I've always believed that Hoosiers step up for our fellow Americans when times get tough, and it's more important than ever that we extend a helping hand to the people of Texas and the Gulf Coast. The road to recovery will be long and difficult, but with your help, I know we can make a difference. Let's show them that we have their back.

If you can, **[please consider making a donation to the Red Cross to help with the Hurricane Harvey relief efforts.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c27a3/7377f838/37cf4f49/51fbc39a/3006776971/VEsH/ "https://www.redcross.org/donate/hurricane-harvey")**

Thank you so much for your help in making sure families in Houston and the Gulf Coast don't weather this storm alone.

Sincerely,

Joe |
---|---|---

|
---
